facts: DLT (dose-limiting toxicity) is a protocol-prespecified adverse event of defined severity at least possibly drug-related, observed within the DLT-evaluation window: typically the first cycle (~28 days) in onco Phase 1, but commonly 7-14 days per dose / per period in healthy-volunteer SAD/MAD clin-pharm studies. The SRC/SMC/DSMB and medical monitor make the DLT determination at the formal cohort safety review; the AI only flags candidate DLTs EARLY so the cohort review is fully prepared.

Dose-escalation design dictates the participant-level stopping math: 3+3 (escalate if 0/3, expand to 6 if 1/3, stop/de-escalate if >=2/cohort with DLT); model-assisted BOIN and mTPI-2 (Bayesian interval rules around a target DLT rate, often ~25-30%); and model-based CRM (continual reassessment of the dose-toxicity curve). Accelerated titration permits single-subject cohorts until a DLT or grade-2 event. The participant DLT count feeds the cohort rule but the rule itself fires at the study tier.

Sentinel dosing in FIH (MHRA/EMA): 1-2 sentinel subjects per cohort are dosed and observed (often 24-48h+) before the remainder; an unfavorable sentinel response is a hard stop-the-cohort early-warning before the rest are dosed.

Individual safety thresholds are graded by CTCAE v5.0; classic clin-pharm DLT triggers include any Grade >=3 non-hematologic, Grade 4 hematologic (or febrile neutropenia / prolonged Grade-4 neutropenia/thrombocytopenia), plus any AE that triggers a protocol individual stopping rule regardless of grade.

Hy's Law DILI signal = ALT or AST >3x ULN AND total bilirubin >2x ULN AND ALP <2x ULN (no cholestasis) AND no alternative cause; this is a serious DILI early-warning. Component/eDISH triggers (ALT >3x/5x/8x/10x ULN, etc.) escalate before a full Hy's-Law pattern appears.

Cardiac safety per ICH E14/E20: individual QTcF >500 ms, or a change-from-baseline (delta-QTcF) >60 ms, are standard alert thresholds (>450/480 ms and delta >30 ms are watch flags), alongside new arrhythmia, >25% PR/QRS change, or clinically significant ECG morphology change.

PK exposure outliers are early warnings of toxicity risk: individual Cmax/AUC exceeding the prespecified safety-margin / NOAEL-scaled exposure cap (multiples of the no-observed-adverse-effect-level exposure), supratherapeutic exposure vs cohort, or unexpected accumulation (Racc) across MAD dosing days exceeding the predicted ratio.

HYBRID ROUTING & GOVERNANCE: every PARTICIPANT-level signal is subject-level safety/PK/unblinded data, so it is processed by the ON-DEVICE LOCAL LLM with ZERO egress (never to cloud Copilot). The local model DETECTS, AGGREGATES, TRIAGES, and DRAFTS the alert; it never adjudicates the DLT/safety decision. Reported safety numbers come from the validated safety database, not the LLM. 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail + ALCOA++ on every flag and human disposition.

signals.name: Candidate DLT in the DLT-evaluation window
signals.signal: An emerging adverse event in a dosed subject that meets the protocol DLT definition (CTCAE grade + attribution at least possibly related) while still inside the active DLT window for that dose/period
signals.data_source: Validated safety DB + EDC AE/CM eCRF; IXRS for dose/cohort assignment; protocol DLT-criteria table
signals.detection: Rule-based: local LLM matches AE grade/term/onset-day/attribution against the prespecified DLT criteria for the assigned dose and flags candidate DLTs in near-real-time; it pre-assembles the case narrative and the running cohort DLT tally for the SRC. The validated safety DB holds the authoritative numbers; the LLM never adjudicates.
signals.threshold: Any AE meeting the protocol DLT definition (e.g. Grade >=3 non-heme, Grade 4 heme, febrile neutropenia, or any individual-stopping-rule AE) within the DLT window for that dose; design-specific (3+3 / BOIN / mTPI-2 / CRM / accelerated titration)
signals.action: Immediate flag to medical monitor and SRC/SMC chair ahead of the formal cohort review; pause further dosing/escalation per protocol pending adjudication
signals.severity: RED - a confirmed DLT can halt escalation, define the MTD early, or terminate the dose level/cohort

signals.name: Sentinel-subject adverse response (FIH)
signals.signal: An unfavorable safety response in a sentinel subject during the mandated sentinel observation window before the rest of the cohort is dosed
signals.data_source: Validated safety DB + EDC; bedside vitals/telemetry; IXRS sentinel flag; protocol sentinel-dosing rule
signals.detection: Rule-based + model-assisted: local LLM watches sentinel-subject AE/vital/lab streams against the cohort-release criteria and raises a stop-the-cohort flag the moment a sentinel breach appears, before remaining subjects are dosed.
signals.threshold: Any clinically significant AE / vital-sign / lab change in a sentinel subject within the protocol sentinel window that breaches the cohort-release criteria
signals.action: Halt dosing of remaining cohort subjects; notify PI and medical monitor immediately; convene SRC before proceeding
signals.severity: RED - blocks cohort dosing and can stop the dose level / escalation

signals.name: SAE / death / life-threatening event
signals.signal: Any new serious adverse event, death, or life-threatening (CTCAE Grade 4-5) event in a dosed subject
signals.data_source: Validated safety DB (SAE form) + EDC; PV intake
signals.detection: Rule-based: local LLM detects seriousness criteria / Grade 4-5 / fatal outcome on entry, drafts the expedited-reporting case summary and links it to dose, exposure, and concomitant meds for the medical monitor. Authoritative seriousness/causality determination is human + validated PV system.
signals.threshold: Any SAE, death, or life-threatening (Grade 4/5) event regardless of attribution
signals.action: Immediate medical-monitor and SRC notification; feeds SUSAR/expedited-reporting clock; sponsor + PV informed
signals.severity: RED - a related/unexpected SAE or death can stop the dose level or terminate the study

signals.name: Individual AE cluster / severity-grade escalation
signals.signal: A within-subject pattern of accumulating AEs or a rising CTCAE grade for the same event (e.g. Grade 1->2->3 over consecutive assessments) that precedes a frank DLT
signals.data_source: Validated safety DB + EDC longitudinal AE records (by MedDRA SOC/PT)
signals.detection: Model-assisted: local LLM trends each subject's AE grades over time, clusters related MedDRA terms, and surfaces an upward severity trajectory before any single event crosses the DLT line. Triage/explanation only; no decision.
signals.threshold: Grade escalation across visits for the same PT, or >=2-3 related AEs clustering in one subject, trending toward a DLT-defining grade
signals.action: Early heads-up to medical monitor and SRC ahead of the cohort review; flag subject for intensified monitoring
signals.severity: AMBER - precursor pattern; can convert to a DLT and gate escalation

signals.name: Hy's Law / drug-induced liver injury signal
signals.signal: Hepatocellular injury pattern indicating potential severe DILI in an individual subject
signals.data_source: Central-lab LFT feed (ALT, AST, total bilirubin, ALP) + EDC; baseline normals
signals.detection: Rule-based (eDISH logic): local LLM evaluates ALT/AST, total bilirubin, ALP against ULN multiples per subject, flags Hy's-Law pattern and component breaches, and assembles the eDISH plot context. Confirmatory repeat-test and adjudication are human + validated lab/safety DB.
signals.threshold: ALT or AST >3x ULN AND total bilirubin >2x ULN AND ALP <2x ULN with no alternative cause (Hy's Law); component watch-flags at ALT >3x/5x/8x ULN
signals.action: Urgent medical-monitor alert; close monitoring / repeat LFTs per protocol; subject stopping rule and SRC review
signals.severity: RED - a Hy's-Law case is a serious DILI signal that can stop dosing for the subject and the program

signals.name: QTcF prolongation / ECG abnormality
signals.signal: Clinically significant QTc prolongation or new conduction/morphology abnormality on individual ECG
signals.data_source: Central ECG core-lab feed (QTcF, PR, QRS, morphology) + EDC; baseline ECG
signals.detection: Rule-based per ICH E14: local LLM compares QTcF and delta-QTcF against thresholds, flags new arrhythmia / conduction change, and links the finding to PK exposure timing. Cardiologist/core-lab read is authoritative.
signals.threshold: QTcF >500 ms OR delta-QTcF >60 ms from baseline (watch at >450/480 ms or delta >30 ms); new clinically significant arrhythmia or >25% PR/QRS change
signals.action: Alert medical monitor / cardiac safety reviewer; subject ECG stopping rule; SRC review before next dose
signals.severity: RED at >500 ms / >60 ms delta (potential individual stopping rule and escalation halt); AMBER at watch thresholds

signals.name: PK exposure outlier vs safety-margin / NOAEL cap
signals.signal: An individual subject's drug exposure exceeds the prespecified safety-margin or NOAEL-scaled exposure limit, signaling overexposure-driven toxicity risk
signals.data_source: Bioanalytical/PK dataset (Cmax, AUC) + EDC dosing; NOAEL-scaled exposure cap from the IB/preclinical package
signals.detection: Statistical: local LLM screens each subject's Cmax/AUC against the prespecified exposure cap and against the cohort distribution (outlier detection), flagging supratherapeutic exposure even before an AE manifests. Validated PK software/pharmacometrician owns the formal NCA values.
signals.threshold: Individual Cmax or AUC exceeding the NOAEL-scaled safety-margin cap, or a statistical outlier vs cohort (e.g. > predefined SD/percentile)
signals.action: Flag to clinical pharmacologist / medical monitor and SRC; informs go/no-go on the next dose level
signals.severity: AMBER/RED - overexposure can gate escalation and trigger early dose-cap or stopping

signals.name: Unexpected accumulation across MAD dosing
signals.signal: Greater-than-predicted accumulation of drug exposure over repeat dosing days in a MAD subject
signals.data_source: Bioanalytical/PK trough + profile data across dosing days + EDC; predicted accumulation ratio from SAD/modeling
signals.detection: Statistical: local LLM tracks each subject's trough/exposure trajectory and accumulation ratio (Racc) against the model-predicted ratio, flagging nonlinear or supra-predicted accumulation that raises steady-state safety risk.
signals.threshold: Observed accumulation ratio exceeding the predicted Racc by the prespecified margin, or rising troughs without plateau (nonlinear PK)
signals.action: Alert pharmacometrician / medical monitor; reassess MAD dose/regimen and steady-state exposure cap with SRC
signals.severity: AMBER - can force regimen change or stop the MAD cohort

signals.name: Vital-sign / hemodynamic event
signals.signal: Clinically significant hemodynamic or vital-sign change (hypotension/hypertension, bradycardia/tachycardia, orthostasis, hypoxia, fever) in a dosed subject
signals.data_source: EDC vitals + bedside telemetry/orthostatics; validated safety DB
signals.detection: Rule-based + model-assisted: local LLM compares vitals against protocol alert bands and baseline, detects post-dose hemodynamic shifts time-locked to dosing, and surfaces the trend for the medical monitor.
signals.threshold: Vital signs breaching protocol clinically-significant thresholds (e.g. SBP <90 / >180 mmHg, HR <40 / >120 bpm, SpO2 <92%, temp >=38.5C) or a protocol vital-sign stopping rule
signals.action: Notify PI / medical monitor; apply subject stopping rule; SRC review if recurrent across the cohort
signals.severity: AMBER/RED depending on severity and recurrence; can trigger individual stopping and inform cohort review

signals.name: Immunogenicity / infusion (hypersensitivity) reaction
signals.signal: Acute infusion-related/hypersensitivity reaction or emerging anti-drug-antibody (ADA) signal, especially for biologics
signals.data_source: EDC AE + infusion-reaction CRF; central-lab ADA/immunogenicity assay; validated safety DB
signals.detection: Rule-based: local LLM flags reactions time-locked to infusion (rash, bronchospasm, hypotension, fever) and links emerging ADA-positive results to clinical events; assembles the case for review.
signals.threshold: Grade >=2 infusion/hypersensitivity reaction, anaphylaxis (any grade), or treatment-emergent ADA positivity associated with a clinical event or PK loss
signals.action: Immediate medical-monitor alert; premedication/rate-change or stop per protocol; SRC review
signals.severity: RED for anaphylaxis/severe reaction (subject stop + program signal); AMBER for milder/asymptomatic ADA

signals.name: Individual protocol stopping-criteria breach
signals.signal: A subject crosses any prespecified individual stopping/withdrawal criterion that mandates halting that subject's dosing
signals.data_source: EDC + validated safety DB + labs/ECG/vitals; protocol individual-stopping-rule table
signals.detection: Rule-based: local LLM continuously evaluates each subject's safety data against the full protocol individual-stopping-criteria checklist and fires the moment any single criterion is met, regardless of whether it also qualifies as a DLT.
signals.threshold: Any breach of the protocol-defined individual stopping/withdrawal criteria (the specific lab/ECG/vital/AE limits listed in the protocol)
signals.action: Immediate flag to PI / medical monitor; stop that subject's dosing per protocol; document and inform SRC
signals.severity: RED for the subject; contributes to cohort-level escalation decision

signals.name: Subject withdrawal / dropout / early discontinuation
signals.signal: A DLT-evaluable subject withdraws, is lost to follow-up, or discontinues before completing the DLT window, jeopardizing DLT-evaluability of the cohort
signals.data_source: EDC disposition/EOT records; IXRS; visit-attendance data
signals.detection: Rule-based: local LLM tracks disposition vs the DLT window and flags subjects who become non-DLT-evaluable (incomplete window), which can require replacement to keep the cohort decision valid.
signals.threshold: Withdrawal / discontinuation / lost-to-follow-up before completing the DLT-evaluation window, dropping evaluable n below the design requirement
signals.action: Alert PM and medical monitor; trigger subject replacement per protocol to preserve cohort DLT-evaluability
signals.severity: AMBER - threatens cohort decision validity and can delay or invalidate the dose-level call

signals.name: Dosing non-compliance / PK-affecting deviation
signals.signal: A dosing error, missed/extra dose, wrong-dose administration, or food/timing deviation that compromises a subject's safety or PK interpretability
signals.data_source: EDC dose-administration + deviation log; eCRF; bioanalytical sampling-time records
signals.detection: Rule-based + model-assisted: local LLM cross-checks actual vs planned dose, timing, and sampling windows, flags non-compliance and deviations that affect exposure or DLT attribution, and drafts the deviation note.
signals.threshold: Any dosing deviation (wrong dose/time/route, missed/double dose) or sampling-window breach that affects safety or PK evaluability of the subject
signals.action: Flag to PM / medical monitor and clinical pharmacologist; assess impact on DLT-evaluability and exposure; log deviation
signals.severity: AMBER - affects data integrity and can confound the dose-level safety/PK decision

signals.name: Eligibility violation / enrolled-in-error
signals.signal: A dosed subject is found to violate inclusion/exclusion criteria, raising a safety-exposure and data-integrity concern
signals.data_source: EDC screening/eligibility eCRF; central-lab screening labs; medical history
signals.detection: Rule-based: local LLM re-checks screening data and post-enrollment findings against the I/E criteria and flags eligibility violations in already-dosed subjects (e.g. a screening lab/ECG/medical-history exclusion missed at enrollment).
signals.threshold: Confirmed inclusion/exclusion breach in an enrolled-and-dosed subject (e.g. exclusionary lab/ECG/comorbidity/con-med present at dosing)
signals.action: Immediate notification to PI / medical monitor; protocol-deviation report; assess subject safety and DLT-evaluability impact; SRC awareness
signals.severity: AMBER/RED - a safety-relevant eligibility breach can require subject withdrawal and signals site/quality risk

signals.name: Wasserstein drift vs pooled reference
signals.signal: Continuous geometry-aware precursor: how far a subject's evolving LFT/ECG/PK distribution has moved from the pooled reference (a Wasserstein barycenter of prior cohorts) — rises before any rule threshold is crossed.
signals.data_source: central-lab / ECG / PK streams (on-device)
signals.detection: Optimal Transport: (sliced-)Wasserstein distance, subject vs reference; the transport map localizes WHICH grades/exposures drive the shift. Decision-support — flags for review, makes no determination.
signals.threshold: W-distance leaves its control band, trending toward worsening (complements the rule-based DLT/Hy's-Law triggers)
signals.action: Early flag to medical monitor; intensify monitoring on the implicated measures
signals.severity: AMBER — continuous precursor, earlier than a threshold rule

signals.name: Free-energy spike (participant generative model)
signals.signal: Bayesian surprise: how unexpected a subject's observations are under the model of their expected trajectory. A spike = the subject is deviating from expectation — anticipatory, before a frank event.
signals.data_source: subject safety/PK observations (on-device)
signals.detection: Active inference: variational free energy (negative log model-evidence), precision-weighted per subject. Decision-support only.
signals.threshold: Free-energy exceeds its rolling band
signals.action: Flag for medical-monitor review; raise the subject's monitoring precision

signals.name: EFE-recommended monitoring action (active sampling)
signals.signal: The agent computes Expected Free Energy over candidate observations (e.g. an unscheduled LFT/ECG/PK draw) and recommends the one that would most reduce uncertainty about emerging DLT/safety risk — proactive, not reactive.
signals.data_source: subject state + protocol-allowed assessments
signals.detection: Active inference EFE = epistemic (information-gain) + pragmatic (reach the preferred/safe state) value. A SUGGESTION for human approval — never an autonomous clinical or dosing action.
signals.threshold: EFE-optimal action exceeds a value-of-information threshold
signals.action: Recommend the data-collection action to the medical monitor for approval

facts: Dose-escalation termination logic depends on the design: rule-based 3+3 (≥2/3 or ≥2/6 DLTs at a dose = MTD exceeded, prior dose = MTD) vs model-assisted BOIN / mTPI-2 / CRM, which carry explicit escalate / stay / de-escalate / ELIMINATE boundary tables and a Bayesian early-stopping rule (e.g., Pr(toxicity at lowest dose > target) > 0.95 => stop the whole study for excess toxicity). The cohort DLT rate is computed by the validated dose-escalation tool, never the LLM; the SRC/SMC owns the escalation decision.

ICH E6(R3) (adopted 6 Jan 2025, effective mid-2025) reframes Quality Tolerance Limits as one example of pre-specified 'acceptable ranges' supporting control of risks to Critical-to-Quality (CtQ) factors; a QTL is a STUDY-level (not site-level) parameter and a breach triggers a documented root-cause assessment and possible protocol/process change — it is a quality-management trigger, not an automatic stop. KRIs operate at site/operational level beneath QTLs.

Central Statistical Monitoring (CSM), a core RBQM method, runs unsupervised/statistical tests across all EDC data to surface data-integrity anomalies: digit preference, terminal-digit rounding, variance too low/high vs other sites, mean shifts, impossible dates (weekends/holidays), odd correlation structure, and under-reporting (a site with far fewer AEs than the study mean) — all classic fabrication/fraud and quality signals. CSM is a validated statistical engine; the LLM only narrates and triages its output.

Aggregate safety-trend monitoring compares observed AE/SAE rates against the background/expected rate from the IB, prior cohorts, and comparator data (NOT a per-subject DLT call); emerging cohort-level lab shifts (e.g., clustering of transaminase elevations, eosinophilia) and ECG shifts (mean QTcF change, cohort outliers) are aggregate emerging-safety-profile signals reviewed by the SRC/DSMB and pharmacovigilance, with reported numbers always from the validated safety database.

Recruitment KRIs are tracked vs the enrollment plan: actual-vs-planned randomization/dosing curve, screen-failure rate vs assumption, screen-to-enrollment ratio, site activation lag, and slot/cohort fill time; sustained enrollment lag against critical-path milestones is a leading futility/feasibility and timeline-slip indicator for early termination or de-scope.

Operational data-quality KRIs include query rate per page/per subject and query aging (days open), missing/overdue CRF pages, time-to-data-entry lag, protocol-deviation rate and trend, and IXRS/IWRS randomization or drug-assignment errors — these are de-identified aggregate metrics suitable for cloud KRI dashboards; any subject-level drill-down moves to the on-device local model.

Hybrid routing for this tier: de-identified AGGREGATE KRIs / QTL status / enrollment & timeline / budget burn are non-sensitive and run on CLOUD M365 Copilot Premium; any drill-down into subject-level safety/PK/unblinded data routes to the ON-DEVICE LOCAL LLM (zero egress). The AI detects, aggregates, triages and drafts the alert; validated tools + DSMB/SRC + the medical monitor own safety/DLT calls and the PM/sponsor own study/contract calls. 21 CFR Part 11 audit + ALCOA++ throughout.

Probability of meeting the primary objective (FIH: characterize safety/tolerability, establish MTD/RP2D and PK) is assessed via the dose-escalation model's projected information (e.g., are cohorts converging on an MTD or escalating without a tolerability signal) plus conditional-power/predictive-probability where a formal endpoint exists; persistent low predicted success or an MTD reached far below the target exposure is a futility / non-viability early warning escalated to the sponsor and DSMB.

signals.name: Cohort DLT rate breaches dose-escalation stopping/de-escalation rule
signals.signal: Observed cohort DLT count/rate at the current dose meets the design's de-escalation or hard-stop boundary (excess toxicity), indicating MTD exceeded / escalation must stop or the study terminate for toxicity
signals.data_source: Validated dose-escalation tool fed by the safety database + DLT adjudication log; EDC/eCRF DLT window data
signals.detection: Rule-based (3+3: ≥2/3 or ≥2/6 DLTs) or model-assisted (BOIN/mTPI-2/CRM boundary table; Bayesian early-stop Pr(tox at lowest dose>target)>0.95). The VALIDATED tool computes the rate and boundary; the local LLM only flags that a boundary is approaching/met, drafts the SRC pre-read, and explains contributing cases — it never declares the DLT or the stop.
signals.engine: On-device local LLM (subject-level DLT drill-down, zero egress) summarizing validated dose-escalation-tool output
signals.threshold: DLT count at dose ≥ the design's de-escalation/elimination/stop boundary, or Bayesian stop probability exceeded; also an 'amber' pre-alert at one DLT short of boundary
signals.action: Immediate escalation to SRC/SMC and medical monitor for the dose-escalation decision (hold escalation / de-escalate / declare MTD / stop); DSMB notified
signals.severity: RED — direct excess-toxicity termination / early-MTD risk; highest-priority safety stop signal

signals.name: Aggregate AE/SAE rate vs background/expected
signals.signal: Pooled AE or SAE incidence (and serious/severe-grade fraction) across active cohorts trends above the IB/expected background or prior-cohort rate — emerging unfavorable safety profile beyond individual DLTs
signals.data_source: Validated safety database (aggregate); MedDRA-coded AE tables; IB/background reference rates
signals.detection: Statistical trend test (observed-vs-expected, CUSUM/Poisson rate comparison) by the validated safety analytics; local LLM aggregates, narrates the emerging-profile shift and drafts the benefit-risk pre-read. Reported counts always come from the validated safety DB, never the LLM.
signals.engine: On-device local LLM (subject-level safety aggregation, zero egress) over validated safety-DB output
signals.threshold: Aggregate SAE/AE rate exceeding the protocol/charter expected band or a significant upward CUSUM signal vs prior cohorts/background
signals.action: Escalate to medical monitor / SRC / DSMB and pharmacovigilance for benefit-risk review
signals.severity: RED/AMBER — emerging-safety-profile deterioration; potential safety termination

signals.name: Cohort lab / ECG (QTcF) shift
signals.signal: Cohort-level clustering of lab abnormalities (e.g., transaminase/bilirubin elevations, eosinophilia, renal markers) or a mean/outlier QTcF prolongation shift — an aggregate organ-safety or cardiac signal
signals.data_source: Central-lab feed + ECG core-lab (validated), aggregated by cohort/dose
signals.detection: Statistical: cohort mean shift, proportion above CTCAE/regulatory thresholds (e.g., QTcF >450/480/500 ms, ΔQTcF >60 ms), Hy's-Law-pattern aggregation. Validated lab/ECG analytics compute; local LLM clusters and explains the dose-response trend.
signals.engine: On-device local LLM (subject-level lab/ECG drill-down, zero egress) over validated lab/ECG output
signals.threshold: Cohort proportion or mean exceeding pre-defined organ-safety/QTcF thresholds, or a dose-response trend across cohorts
signals.action: Escalate to medical monitor / SRC / DSMB; cardiac-safety or hepatic-safety review
signals.severity: AMBER/RED — organ-toxicity or cardiac-safety signal that can halt escalation

signals.name: Safety Quality Tolerance Limit (QTL) breach
signals.signal: A study-level safety/critical CtQ parameter crosses its pre-specified QTL 'acceptable range' (e.g., proportion of critical safety data missing, SAE-underreporting indicator, key endpoint completeness) signaling systemic reliability risk to participant safety or trial results
signals.data_source: Aggregate EDC + safety DB QTL metrics; RBQM/CSM platform (de-identified)
signals.detection: Rule-based threshold crossing per the ICH E6(R3) RBQM plan; cloud Copilot flags the breach, drafts the documented root-cause/CAPA narrative and routes it. QTL is a quality trigger, not an auto-stop.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (de-identified aggregate QTL metric)
signals.threshold: QTL parameter outside its pre-specified acceptable range (with secondary/warning limit for early alert)
signals.action: Escalate to PM / quality lead / sponsor for root-cause assessment and possible protocol/process change; document per E6(R3)
signals.severity: AMBER — systemic data-reliability/safety-quality risk; reputational + reliability termination risk

signals.name: Enrollment lag vs plan (recruitment KRI)
signals.signal: Actual cumulative randomized/dosed subjects fall persistently below the planned recruitment curve; slow site activation and slot/cohort fill — leading feasibility & timeline-slip indicator
signals.data_source: CTMS / IXRS-IWRS enrollment feed; recruitment plan (de-identified counts)
signals.detection: Rule-based actual-vs-planned variance + trend/forecast; cloud Copilot computes the gap, projects completion date and drafts the recruitment-risk brief.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (de-identified aggregate enrollment counts)
signals.threshold: Cumulative enrollment < X% of plan for ≥N weeks, or projected last-subject-in slips past the critical-path milestone
signals.action: Escalate to PM and sponsor; feasibility/site-add or de-scope discussion

signals.name: Screen-failure / screen-to-enrollment ratio breach
signals.signal: Screen-failure rate exceeds the planning assumption (eligibility too tight, population scarce), inflating cost/time and threatening feasibility
signals.data_source: CTMS / EDC screening logs; IXRS (de-identified aggregate ratios)
signals.detection: Rule-based ratio vs assumption + trend; cloud Copilot trends the ratio and identifies driving eligibility criteria from de-identified screen-fail reasons.
signals.threshold: Screen-failure rate above the protocol-assumed band, or worsening trend across cohorts
signals.action: Escalate to PM / medical monitor; consider protocol amendment to eligibility

signals.name: Futility / low probability of meeting the primary objective
signals.signal: Projected inability to achieve the FIH objective — e.g., no MTD/RP2D path within the dose range, MTD reached far below target exposure, or low conditional/predictive power for a defined endpoint — non-viability signal
signals.data_source: Validated dose-escalation model + PK exposure summaries; SAP interim analysis (de-identified projections)
signals.detection: Model-assisted: dose-escalation model projection / conditional-power / predictive-probability from the validated tool; local LLM narrates the futility scenario where subject-level exposure/safety is involved.
signals.engine: On-device local LLM for subject-level exposure/safety drill-down; Cloud Copilot for de-identified aggregate projections
signals.threshold: Predicted primary-objective success below the pre-specified futility bound, or MTD/exposure trajectory off-target
signals.action: Escalate to DSMB/SRC and sponsor for futility/continue decision
signals.severity: RED/AMBER — futility / program non-viability termination risk

signals.name: Query rate & query-aging KRI
signals.signal: Elevated query rate per subject/page or growing backlog of aged open queries — data-cleaning and reliability risk that delays database lock
signals.data_source: EDC query management (de-identified aggregate metrics)
signals.detection: Rule-based KRI thresholds + aging buckets; cloud Copilot trends rate/aging by site and drafts the data-management escalation.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (de-identified aggregate query metrics)
signals.threshold: Query rate above KRI limit or % queries open >X days above threshold / rising trend
signals.action: Escalate to data management lead / PM; site follow-up

signals.name: Missing-data & overdue-CRF trend
signals.signal: Rising proportion of missing critical data points / overdue eCRF pages or data-entry lag, threatening completeness of the safety and PK datasets
signals.data_source: EDC completeness metrics (de-identified aggregate)
signals.detection: Rule-based completeness KRI + trend; cloud Copilot flags critical-variable gaps and drafts the completeness alert.
signals.threshold: Missing critical-data % or data-entry lag above KRI limit / upward trend; ties to a safety QTL when critical
signals.action: Escalate to data management / PM; if safety-critical, medical monitor

signals.name: Protocol-deviation rate & trend
signals.signal: Rising rate of protocol deviations (especially important/critical: dosing errors, eligibility, IP-handling, visit-window) — site-conduct and data-reliability risk
signals.data_source: CTMS / EDC deviation log (de-identified aggregate; severity-coded)
signals.detection: Rule-based KRI + trend with severity weighting; cloud Copilot trends and clusters deviation types, drafts the GCP-risk brief. Important-deviation clustering can feed a QTL.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (de-identified aggregate deviation metrics)
signals.threshold: Critical/important deviation rate above KRI limit or rising trend, or a single critical safety deviation
signals.action: Escalate to PM / quality / medical monitor; CAPA, possible for-cause review

signals.name: IXRS / randomization & drug-assignment integrity
signals.signal: IXRS/IWRS errors — mis-randomization, wrong dose/kit assignment, unblinding events, dispensing errors — compromising safety, integrity, and supply tracking
signals.data_source: IXRS/IWRS transaction logs + drug-accountability (de-identified aggregate; subject-level for the specific event)
signals.detection: Rule-based anomaly detection on transactions; cloud Copilot flags aggregate error trend, local LLM handles any subject-level/unblinding drill-down.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (aggregate transaction anomalies); On-device local LLM for subject-level/unblinding events
signals.threshold: Any unblinding/wrong-dose dispensing event, or IXRS error rate above threshold
signals.action: Escalate to PM / medical monitor (safety) and quality; possible for-cause

signals.name: Central statistical-monitoring data-integrity anomaly
signals.signal: CSM detects fabrication/quality anomalies: digit preference, terminal-digit rounding, variance too low/high, mean shifts, impossible/holiday dates, odd correlations, or AE/lab UNDER-reporting vs the study mean — site fraud or systemic data-integrity risk
signals.data_source: RBQM/CSM platform over all EDC data (validated statistical engine; de-identified aggregate site profiles)
signals.detection: Statistical (validated CSM): unsupervised multivariate tests, digit-preference & variance tests, site-outlier profiling. The CSM tool computes the signal; cloud Copilot narrates and prioritizes the flagged sites, drafts the central-monitoring report — it does not adjudicate fraud.
signals.engine: Validated tool (CSM statistical engine) + human; Cloud M365 Copilot narrates de-identified site-anomaly output
signals.threshold: CSM anomaly score / p-value past the platform threshold for a site or variable cluster; under-reporting flag vs study mean
signals.action: Escalate to quality / biostatistics / PM; targeted SDV or for-cause audit
signals.severity: AMBER/RED — data-integrity / fraud termination & reliability risk

signals.name: Site / operational performance outlier
signals.signal: A site is a multi-metric outlier (slow enrollment, high deviations, high query/aging, slow data entry, AE under/over-reporting) — concentrated conduct risk that can jeopardize study reliability
signals.data_source: CTMS + EDC + IXRS composite site scorecard (de-identified aggregate)
signals.detection: Model-assisted site-profiling / weighted composite KRI ranking; cloud Copilot ranks sites, explains the driving metrics, drafts the site-management plan.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (de-identified aggregate site scorecards)
signals.threshold: Composite site-risk score above threshold or KRI breaches on ≥N metrics
signals.action: Escalate to PM / CRA lead; targeted monitoring, retraining, or site closure

signals.name: Timeline slip & budget-burn vs milestones
signals.signal: Critical-path milestones (FPI, LPI, last-subject-last-visit, DBL) projected to slip, and/or budget burn diverging from milestone progress (over-burn or scope creep) — sponsor de-scope/cancel pressure
signals.data_source: CTMS / Smartsheet project plan + finance (de-identified milestone & cost metrics)
signals.detection: Rule-based earned-value / schedule-variance + forecast; cloud Copilot computes schedule & cost variance, projects end-date and burn, drafts the program-risk brief.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive project & finance metrics)
signals.threshold: Critical milestone forecast past baseline by >X, or cost-to-progress variance beyond threshold
signals.action: Escalate to PM / program lead and sponsor; replan / change-order discussion

signals.name: SUSAR / expedited-reporting clock breach
signals.signal: An event assessed as serious, unexpected, and related (SUSAR) is approaching or has breached the regulatory expedited-reporting clock — 7 calendar days for fatal/life-threatening, 15 days for other SUSARs (ICH E2A / 21 CFR 312.32 IND safety reports; EU CT Reg). Tracks time-elapsed since investigator awareness vs the regulatory deadline, plus un-submitted/at-risk SUSAR backlog.
signals.engine: On-device local (subject-level unblinded causality/seriousness) feeding a de-identified count/clock to cloud for ops escalation
signals.data_source: Safety database / PV system (validated); investigator awareness timestamps
signals.detection: Clock countdown vs 7/15-day deadline; backlog of un-reconciled serious events pending causality/expectedness assessment
signals.threshold: Alert at T-minus-X days before 7- or 15-day deadline; any breach = critical
signals.action: Draft expedited-report status alert to PV/medical monitor and regulatory; never auto-submit or auto-assess causality

signals.name: Clinical hold / partial hold / IND safety-report regulatory action
signals.signal: FDA clinical hold, partial clinical hold, or other competent-authority action (EMA/MHRA), or an emerging issue likely to trigger one (e.g., pattern meeting IND-safety-report criteria under 21 CFR 312.32(c) — aggregate serious findings, increased rate/severity of anticipated serious events). Distinct from internal study stopping rules; this is the regulator halting dosing.
signals.engine: Cloud (regulatory correspondence/portal status, public hold notices) + on-device for the safety-aggregate trigger pattern
signals.data_source: Regulatory correspondence inbox, FDA/agency portals, sponsor regulatory team; safety aggregate from validated DB
signals.detection: Detect hold notice receipt; or aggregate-serious-event pattern crossing IND-safety-report logic
signals.threshold: Any hold/partial-hold = critical; aggregate pattern = warning to medical monitor
signals.action: Draft regulatory-action alert; flag dosing-pause implications; humans own filing and hold response

signals.name: DSMB / IDMC / SRC recommendation-to-stop or pause
signals.signal: An independent DSMB/IDMC or internal Safety Review Committee issues a recommendation to stop, pause, modify, or not-escalate — or its recommendation is overdue/diverging from sponsor intent. Captures the formal recommendation event and any sponsor non-concurrence (a known termination precursor).
signals.engine: On-device local (recommendations reference unblinded safety) with de-identified status to ops
signals.data_source: DSMB/IDMC charter outputs, SRC minutes, recommendation letters
signals.detection: Recommendation = stop/pause/modify/hold-escalation; or sponsor non-adoption of a safety recommendation
signals.threshold: Any stop/pause recommendation = critical; modify/no-escalate = warning
signals.action: Draft governance-recommendation alert and route to PM/sponsor; committee owns the recommendation, sponsor owns the decision

signals.name: DSMB/SRC meeting-cadence & dose-escalation-decision data-package readiness
signals.signal: Scheduled safety/dose-escalation review (DSMB, SRC, or cohort dose-escalation meeting / SMC) is overdue, lacks quorum, or its required data package (cleaned safety/PK through the DLT window) is not locked/ready by the gating deadline — delaying the go/no-go to dose, expand, or escalate.
signals.data_source: Committee charter cadence, meeting scheduler, data-cleaning/lock status of the cohort package
signals.detection: Days-to-meeting vs data-readiness; missed/no-quorum meeting; package incompleteness at the gate
signals.threshold: Package not ready by T-minus-N business days = warning; missed gating meeting = critical (blocks escalation)
signals.action: Draft readiness/cadence alert to data management and PM; humans schedule and decide

signals.name: Aggregate benefit-risk / emerging-safety-profile shift (DSUR-style)
signals.signal: A trend-level shift in the emerging cumulative safety profile not visible in any single event — rising incidence/severity of a specific AE term, a newly emerging organ-system signal, shifting laboratory or ECG central tendencies across cohorts, or a benefit-risk inflection. The aggregate equivalent of the periodic DSUR/aggregate-safety review, run continuously.
signals.engine: On-device local (unblinded/blinded subject-level safety aggregation; zero egress)
signals.data_source: Validated safety database, central labs/ECG, cumulative exposure
signals.detection: Disproportionality / trend on cumulative MedDRA-coded events vs prior cohorts and reference; central-tendency drift
signals.threshold: Statistically/clinically meaningful upward shift in a term cluster or organ system
signals.action: Draft emerging-profile summary for medical monitor / aggregate-safety review; never re-characterize benefit-risk autonomously

signals.name: Bioanalytical assay failure / sample-integrity loss affecting PK
signals.signal: PK (or biomarker/ADA) data are compromised by assay failure, ISR (incurred-sample-reanalysis) failure, run rejection, calibration/QC failure, sample mishandling, chain-of-custody/temperature-excursion breach, hemolysis, or loss in transit — undermining the exposure data the dose-escalation decision depends on.
signals.data_source: Bioanalytical lab LIMS, sample-accountability/courier logs, QC/ISR reports
signals.detection: Run-rejection rate, QC/calibration failures, ISR pass-rate breach, missing/excursion samples per cohort
signals.threshold: ISR pass-rate < acceptance (e.g., regulatory ISR criteria), or assay-run rejection / sample-loss above tolerance for a gating cohort
signals.action: Draft PK-integrity alert to clinical pharmacology/PK scientist; flag escalation-decision risk; humans adjudicate data usability

signals.name: Investigational product supply: stock-out / expiry / shipment & cold-chain failure
signals.signal: Risk that dosing stops for non-safety reasons — IMP stock-out projected before next cohort, batch expiry / short remaining shelf-life, failed or delayed drug-product release, temperature/cold-chain excursion quarantining usable stock, recall, or label/randomization-kit shortage at site.
signals.engine: Cloud (depot/IXRS supply ops, logistics) — non-sensitive operational
signals.data_source: IXRS/IRT supply module, depot inventory, QP/QA release status, temperature-monitoring logs
signals.detection: Projected days-of-supply vs enrollment plan; expiry runway; excursion quarantine; release delay
signals.threshold: Projected supply < lead time to next cohort; remaining shelf-life < dosing window; any cold-chain quarantine of gating stock
signals.action: Draft supply-risk alert to clinical supply/PM; humans manage resupply and QP release

signals.name: Specialty-vendor / lab / ECG-core / IXRS outage or performance failure
signals.signal: A critical study vendor (central lab, ECG core lab, IXRS/IRT, bioanalytical lab, eCOA/ePRO, courier) degrades or fails — system downtime, result-turnaround breach, data-transfer failure/feed gaps, or for-cause quality issue — blocking safety/PK reads needed for dosing decisions. Distinct from internal site/operational performance.
signals.engine: Cloud (vendor ops, SLA, system status) — non-sensitive operational
signals.data_source: Vendor SLA dashboards, data-transfer/reconciliation logs, helpdesk/incident tickets
signals.detection: Turnaround-time SLA breach, missed/late data feeds, system-uptime drop, reconciliation mismatch count
signals.threshold: SLA breach on a gating deliverable (e.g., safety labs/ECG before dosing review), or feed gap > tolerance
signals.action: Draft vendor-performance alert to vendor manager/PM; humans manage escalation/contingency

signals.name: Informed-consent invalidity / re-consent trigger breach
signals.signal: Consent problems that can halt or invalidate enrollment: new safety information requiring re-consent of active subjects, an expired/superseded ICF version in use, IRB/EC re-approval lapse, missing/invalid consent for a dosed subject, or subjects continuing on an outdated ICF after a safety amendment.
signals.engine: Cloud for version/approval-cadence tracking; on-device if linked to subject-level consent records
signals.data_source: IRB/EC approval records, ICF version control, subject consent log, amendment tracker
signals.detection: ICF version mismatch vs current approved; re-consent not completed within window after new safety info; IRB continuing-review lapse
signals.threshold: Any dosed subject on a superseded ICF, or re-consent overdue past defined window = critical compliance flag
signals.action: Draft consent/re-consent alert to clinical operations/regulatory; humans manage IRB and re-consent

signals.name: Protocol-amendment churn / substantial-amendment frequency
signals.signal: High frequency or rapid succession of protocol amendments — especially substantial/safety-driven ones — as a leading indicator of design instability, unexpected safety findings, or sponsor uncertainty, each carrying re-approval, re-consent, and timeline cost. Tracks amendment count, substantial-vs-administrative mix, and inter-amendment interval.
signals.data_source: Protocol version control, amendment log, IRB/EC submission tracker
signals.detection: Amendments per unit time vs benchmark; share that are substantial/safety-driven; shortening interval
signals.threshold: Amendment rate or substantial-amendment count above benchmark for study phase/duration
signals.action: Draft amendment-churn trend to PM/sponsor; humans assess design and program implications

signals.name: Site for-cause / sponsor / regulatory inspection findings (data integrity / GCP)
signals.signal: Serious GCP or data-integrity findings — for-cause audit, critical/major audit or monitoring findings, FDA Form 483 / inspection observations, PI misconduct, fabrication/falsification signal, or systematic CSM-flagged site anomaly — that can disqualify a site or its data and jeopardize the study/program.
signals.engine: Cloud (audit/inspection ops) + on-device cross-check against subject-level data patterns
signals.data_source: Audit/inspection reports, monitoring CAPA log, central statistical monitoring outputs, regulatory inspection notices
signals.detection: Critical/major finding count, for-cause trigger, fraud-pattern flags from CSM, repeat/un-resolved CAPA
signals.threshold: Any critical finding or for-cause/fraud signal; major-finding accumulation above tolerance
signals.action: Draft inspection/audit-risk alert to QA/PM/sponsor; humans own CAPA and regulatory response

signals.name: Cohort distributional shift across escalating doses (Wasserstein)
signals.signal: W₂ distance between the current cohort's safety/exposure distribution and the prior-cohort barycenter — quantifies the dose-escalation safety shift continuously and is sensitive to TAIL mass moving (the dangerous part), which mean-threshold rules miss.
signals.data_source: aggregate (de-identified) cohort safety/PK
signals.detection: Optimal Transport on aggregate distributions; the transport plan shows which severity grades / exposure ranges drive the shift.
signals.threshold: W-distance trend exceeds the cohort-to-cohort control band
signals.action: Surface to the SRC ahead of the escalation review

signals.name: Study-level free-energy drift (model evidence)
signals.signal: Surprise of aggregate trajectories (enrollment, AE rate, data quality) vs the pooled expected-study model; a sustained rise = the study departing from its expected course.
signals.data_source: aggregate study KRIs
signals.detection: Active inference at the study level; the QTLs encode the 'preferred' states (prior preferences C). Free energy over aggregate states.
signals.threshold: Sustained free-energy rise above band
signals.action: PM review; SRC if safety-driven

signals.name: EFE-guided risk-based monitoring allocation
signals.signal: Precision-weighted EFE recommends which site/cohort to monitor next to most reduce risk uncertainty — anticipatory RBM that focuses effort where information-gain is highest.
signals.data_source: site/cohort KRIs + uncertainty
signals.detection: Active inference precision/EFE ranks monitoring targets; decision-support for the monitoring plan.
signals.threshold: EFE ranking of targets
signals.action: Feed the risk-based monitoring plan; CRA tasking (human-approved)
signals.severity: GREEN — optimization of effort, not a risk itself

signals.name: Site data-distribution anomaly (Optimal Transport)
signals.signal: Wasserstein distance of a site's data distribution from the pooled barycenter flags central-monitoring outliers — digit preference, variance anomalies, possible fabrication.
signals.data_source: per-site aggregate (de-identified) data
signals.detection: OT distance, site vs pool; a large distance = outlier site. Decision-support; triggers for-cause review.
signals.threshold: Site W-distance beyond the pool's tolerance
signals.action: Flag for central-monitoring / for-cause review

facts: This tier is COMMERCIAL early-warning, not clinical: it detects that the SPONSOR may cancel, de-scope, pause/hold, or not renew/extend the program — the risk to the CRO's revenue and backlog, NOT a patient-safety or DLT signal. It is decision-support/triage for the account/BD lead and PM, never a verdict; the human owns the call and acts via relationship management and contract levers.

Data here is overwhelmingly NON-SENSITIVE business/portfolio/public data (CRM, finance/AR, CTMS/Smartsheet milestones, contract metadata, sponsor email/meeting metadata, public SEC/8-K/10-Q filings, press, clinicaltrials.gov, analyst notes), so it routes to CLOUD M365 Copilot — NOT the on-device local model. Carve-outs: any email body containing unblinded/subject-level safety or PK content stays on-device; for sentiment analysis Copilot should ideally read metadata/cadence and de-identified summaries, with privacy/confidentiality and FCPA/insider-trading guardrails on sponsor comms.

Industry benchmarks make several thresholds concrete: CRO backlog cancellations run ~4% of prior-quarter ending backlog as a baseline (a spike above that is the alert); single-sponsor concentration >~10% of backlog is a recognized revenue-disruption risk; book-to-bill <1.0 and falling Net Dollar Retention are renewal-health KRIs. AR aging past Net-30/45/60 contract terms and a rising change-order-disputed ratio are the earliest, hardest financial signals.

Early-phase (Phase 1 FIH SAD/MAD) sponsors skew heavily toward small/emerging biotech funded by venture/equity, so SPONSOR FINANCIAL HEALTH is the dominant termination driver: cash-runway < ~12 months, a going-concern qualification (10-K/10-Q), layoffs/restructuring, a down-round or failed raise, or stock collapse routinely precede program pause/termination regardless of how the trial itself is performing.

PIPELINE/PORTFOLIO reprioritization is the #1 non-financial cause of early-phase cancellation: a failed or disappointing ADJACENT program (same target/MoA/platform), a competitor's readout or approval, a strategic pivot, new-CMO/CSO leadership, or a board decision to concentrate cash on late-stage assets can kill a clean Phase 1 program. These signals come from public readouts, conference abstracts (ASCO/AACR/JPM), press, and 8-Ks — classic Copilot + web territory.

M&A is a step-change risk: an acquisition or reverse-merger of the sponsor triggers portfolio rationalization and change-of-control clauses; the early warning is rumor/announcement/definitive-agreement tracking plus knowing our own contract's change-of-control and termination-for-convenience terms (most CRO master service agreements allow sponsor termination for convenience on ~30–90 days notice, which caps recoverable backlog).

RELATIONSHIP/ENGAGEMENT decay is the leading INTRA-relationship indicator independent of finances: drop in meeting/email cadence, slower response latency, loss of the internal champion, escalations, repeated milestone/invoice disputes, sponsor micromanagement (a trust-erosion tell), and declining sentiment in comms. Copilot triages cadence/sentiment/escalation patterns from CRM + comms metadata; the account lead interprets and re-engages.

CONTRACTUAL timing signals are deterministic and high-value: approaching contract end-date / option-exercise windows / renewal decision dates, un-exercised cohort-expansion or follow-on options, stalled change-order negotiation, and notice-period clocks. These are rule-based countdowns from contract metadata (CTMS/CLM) — the cleanest 'amber-before-red' lead time the account team gets.

Governance: 21 CFR Part 11 / ALCOA++ audit trail and a human-acknowledgement/disposition log apply to every fired alert; reported financial AR/backlog figures come from the validated finance/ERP system of record, not the LLM. The LLM aggregates, triages, explains, and DRAFTS the risk brief / save-the-account memo — it does not decide to escalate, discount, or write off.

signals.name: AR / payment aging breach
signals.signal: Sponsor invoices aging past contract payment terms — a leading sign of budget stress, dissatisfaction-driven withholding, or inability to pay.
signals.data_source: Finance/AR sub-ledger + ERP (system of record), contract payment terms from CLM/CTMS
signals.detection: Rule-based aging buckets computed by the validated finance system; Copilot AGGREGATES across invoices, correlates with disputes/cadence, and DRAFTS the AR-risk brief. Reported dollar figures come from ERP, never the LLM.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive) + Validated finance system of record
signals.threshold: Any material invoice >60 days past Net-30/45 terms, OR total past-due trending up 2+ consecutive periods, OR a single invoice in dispute >$X
signals.action: Account/BD lead + PM + finance/AR controller; escalate to relationship-governance call
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: hard early signal of de-scope/non-payment/termination risk; oldest, most reliable financial tell

signals.name: Change-order / scope-reduction / hold trend
signals.signal: Pattern of change orders trending toward scope REDUCTION, descoped tasks, paused work packages, or stalled CO sign-off (vs. growth change orders).
signals.data_source: CTMS / Smartsheet / CLM change-order log, contract scope, project plan
signals.detection: Rule-based on CO direction/value + status aging; Copilot classifies CO sentiment (reduce vs. expand), flags stall/hold patterns, and explains. PM validates.
signals.threshold: ≥2 reduction/hold COs in a quarter, OR a CO unsigned/stalled >30 days, OR cumulative scope down vs. baseline contract value
signals.action: Account lead + PM; trigger scope/relationship review with sponsor
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: directional de-scope often precedes full cancellation

signals.name: Cancellation-rate / backlog-erosion spike
signals.signal: This program's contribution to backlog shrinking faster than the industry baseline — early de-scope or quiet wind-down before formal termination notice.
signals.data_source: Backlog/financial-planning system, CTMS revenue forecast, contract value
signals.detection: Statistical vs. benchmark — Copilot compares realized erosion to the ~4%-of-prior-quarter-backlog cancellation baseline and flags excess; finance validates the numbers.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive) + Validated finance system
signals.threshold: Quarterly backlog burn on this program exceeds ~4% baseline cancellation rate, OR forecast revenue revised down >X% unrelated to milestones
signals.action: Account lead + portfolio/finance leadership
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: leading indicator of revenue loss; feeds book-to-bill / NDR

signals.name: Communication cadence drop
signals.signal: Sharp decline in sponsor meeting frequency, email volume, or rising response-latency — disengagement before churn.
signals.data_source: Sponsor comms metadata (email/calendar), CRM activity log, meeting minutes
signals.detection: Statistical anomaly on cadence/latency vs. the relationship's own baseline. Copilot reads METADATA/cadence (not sensitive bodies) and de-identified summaries; flags the drop. Account lead interprets.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive metadata) — sensitive bodies excluded/on-device
signals.threshold: Meeting/email cadence down >40–50% vs. trailing baseline, OR median response latency doubles, OR a governance meeting cancelled/not rescheduled
signals.action: Account/BD lead; proactive re-engagement / health check
signals.severity: AMBER: classic B2B churn precursor; soft but early

signals.name: Sentiment decline in sponsor comms
signals.signal: Tone of sponsor emails/minutes shifting negative — frustration, blame, dissatisfaction, comparisons to other CROs.
signals.data_source: Email/meeting-minutes text (de-identified), CRM notes, escalation log
signals.detection: Model-assisted NLP sentiment/topic on de-identified comms; Copilot trends sentiment and surfaces representative quotes, with confidentiality guardrails. Human reads context — sentiment is noisy, triage-only.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive, de-identified) — never subject-level content
signals.threshold: Sentiment trend negative for 2+ consecutive periods, OR ≥N negative/escalation-flagged messages in a window
signals.action: Account lead; loop in PM/relationship sponsor
signals.severity: AMBER: corroborating signal, weak alone — pair with cadence/disputes

signals.name: Loss of internal champion
signals.signal: Departure, reassignment, or disengagement of the sponsor's key advocate/decision-maker — one of the strongest churn predictors in B2B.
signals.data_source: CRM stakeholder map, comms metadata, LinkedIn/press (public), org changes
signals.detection: Rule-based on stakeholder activity + public role-change detection; Copilot flags champion silence or exit and maps remaining relationship coverage. Account lead confirms.
signals.threshold: Champion comms cease >X weeks, OR public departure/role change, OR new unknown decision-maker inserted
signals.action: Account/BD lead; rebuild multi-threaded relationship
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: champion loss frequently precedes vendor switch

signals.name: Milestone dispute / escalation flag
signals.signal: Disputes over milestone achievement, deliverable acceptance, quality complaints, or formal escalations — trust erosion and contract-leverage moves.
signals.data_source: CTMS milestone log, escalation/issue tracker, meeting minutes, contract
signals.detection: Rule-based on escalation/dispute events; Copilot clusters recurring complaint themes and links to invoice/CO status. PM/quality validate the facts.
signals.threshold: ≥1 formal escalation, OR ≥2 milestone/acceptance disputes in a quarter, OR sponsor micromanagement spike (a trust tell)
signals.action: PM + account lead; quality/governance review
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: disputes are both a cause and a pretext for termination

signals.name: Sponsor pipeline / portfolio reprioritization
signals.signal: Sponsor deprioritizing or shelving this asset/indication — strategic pivot, cash concentration on late-stage, or therapeutic-area exit.
signals.data_source: Public 8-K/press/earnings calls, clinicaltrials.gov status, conference abstracts, CRM intel
signals.detection: Model-assisted web/filing monitoring; Copilot detects pipeline-graphic changes, 'strategic review', 'focusing resources' language, and trial-status flips, then summarizes asset-specific risk. BD lead corroborates.
signals.threshold: Public deprioritization/shelving language for this asset/TA, OR pipeline removal, OR clinicaltrials.gov status → suspended/withdrawn
signals.action: Account/BD lead + portfolio leadership; renewal-defense plan
signals.severity: RED: #1 non-financial cause of early-phase cancellation

signals.name: Sponsor financial health / runway
signals.signal: Emerging-biotech sponsor running low on cash — the dominant termination driver in Phase 1 work.
signals.data_source: Public 10-Q/10-K, 8-K, press, IR decks, analyst notes; (private) news of raises/runway
signals.detection: Model-assisted filing analysis; Copilot extracts cash, burn, and quarters-of-runway, flags going-concern language, and trends. Figures cited to the filing, not invented. Finance/BD validate.
signals.threshold: Cash runway < ~12 months (or below next-milestone date), OR going-concern qualification, OR failed/withdrawn raise, OR debt covenant breach
signals.action: Account/BD lead + finance + leadership; assess pre-payment/credit terms
signals.severity: RED: insolvency-driven termination ignores trial performance

signals.name: Layoffs / restructuring signal
signals.signal: Sponsor announces workforce cuts, restructuring, or R&D/discovery shutdown — cash conservation that usually de-scopes programs.
signals.data_source: Public press / layoff trackers / WARN notices / 8-K / LinkedIn (public)
signals.detection: Rule + model-assisted news monitoring; Copilot detects layoff/restructuring events for the sponsor and links to affected programs. BD lead confirms relevance to our asset.
signals.threshold: Any announced layoff/restructuring/program-cut at the sponsor, especially clinical-ops or our TA
signals.action: Account/BD lead; proactive status check on our program
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: strong corroborator of runway/reprioritization risk

signals.name: Sponsor M&A / acquisition / reverse-merger
signals.signal: Sponsor being acquired, merging, or doing a reverse merger — triggers portfolio rationalization and change-of-control contract clauses.
signals.data_source: Public M&A news/8-K/definitive agreements, rumor trackers, CRM intel, our contract terms (CLM)
signals.detection: Model-assisted news/filing monitoring; Copilot flags rumor→announcement→close and cross-references OUR change-of-control / termination-for-convenience terms. Legal/BD validate.
signals.engine: Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive + public web) + contract metadata
signals.threshold: M&A rumor/announcement/definitive agreement involving the sponsor; change-of-control clause present in our MSA
signals.action: Account/BD lead + legal + leadership; engage acquirer early
signals.severity: RED: step-change; acquirers routinely cut acquired Phase 1 programs

signals.name: Competitive / adjacent-program readout
signals.signal: A competitor readout/approval or a FAILED adjacent program (same target/MoA/platform) that undermines the rationale for this asset.
signals.data_source: Public readouts, ASCO/AACR/JPM abstracts, FDA actions, press, clinicaltrials.gov
signals.detection: Model-assisted web monitoring keyed to target/MoA/indication; Copilot summarizes the competitive event and reasons about spillover risk to our asset. Scientific/BD leads judge relevance.
signals.threshold: Competitor approval or strong readout in same indication, OR adjacent/same-MoA program safety or efficacy failure
signals.action: Account/BD lead + scientific lead; sponsor strategy check-in
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: can trigger a 'why continue?' reprioritization

signals.name: Contract end-date / renewal & option-exercise window
signals.signal: Approaching contract end, renewal-decision date, or un-exercised cohort-expansion/follow-on option — the decision moment for de-scope or non-renewal.
signals.data_source: Contract/CLM metadata, CTMS milestones, option schedule, notice-period clocks
signals.detection: Rule-based deterministic countdown; Copilot tracks days-to-decision and un-exercised options and DRAFTS the renewal-defense brief. PM/account lead own the play.
signals.threshold: <90 days to contract end / renewal / option-exercise date, OR option past its trigger milestone un-exercised
signals.action: Account/BD lead + PM; launch renewal/extension campaign
signals.severity: AMBER: cleanest, earliest deterministic lead time — act before it goes red

signals.name: Relationship governance / KOL-strategy pivot flags
signals.signal: Governance-meeting risk flags (missed/cancelled JSC/JOC, sponsor sourcing-strategy shift to preferred-provider, RFP-shopping signals) and sponsor leadership/KOL strategy pivots (new CMO/CSO, changed development strategy).
signals.data_source: Governance meeting minutes/cadence, CRM intel, public leadership-change news, RFP/procurement signals
signals.detection: Rule + model-assisted; Copilot flags governance-meeting anomalies, detects new sponsor leadership / strategy-pivot language, and surfaces RFP-shopping tells. Account lead interprets intent.
signals.threshold: Cancelled/skipped governance meeting, OR new CMO/CSO/dev-strategy change, OR detected competitive RFP / second-CRO sourcing
signals.action: Account/BD lead + relationship sponsor; executive re-engagement
signals.severity: AMBER→RED: governance + leadership pivots reshape vendor decisions

signals.name: Same-compound / cross-study & class safety signal
signals.signal: A safety signal arising OUTSIDE this study for the same molecule (another trial, nonclinical/tox finding, sponsor's other program) or for the drug class/target (competitor compound toxicity, FDA safety communication, label/class warning, mechanism-based off-target finding) that could halt this study even with a clean in-study profile.
signals.engine: Cloud (literature, FDA safety comms, registries, competitor disclosures); on-device for sponsor-confidential cross-study unblinded findings
signals.data_source: FAERS/FDA safety communications, ClinicalTrials.gov, literature, sponsor cross-study safety, regulatory bulletins
signals.detection: New same-compound/class serious finding, regulatory safety communication, competitor program safety halt for the target/class
signals.threshold: Any credible same-compound or mechanism-class serious safety event
signals.action: Draft cross-study/class safety brief to medical monitor/sponsor; humans assess applicability and action

signals.name: Competitor approval / pivotal readout (positive OR negative) reshaping program value
signals.signal: A competitor's regulatory approval, breakthrough/fast-track grant, or pivotal readout in the same indication/target — positive (a competitor wins the race / lands-grab the market, eroding this program's commercial rationale) or negative (validation collapse / mechanism doubt) — driving a sponsor go/no-go to deprioritize or kill the program.
signals.data_source: FDA/EMA approval trackers, competitor press/IR, ClinicalTrials.gov, conference/abstract readouts, analyst coverage
signals.detection: Competitor approval/designation event; pivotal-trial readout in same indication/target/MoA
signals.threshold: Any first-in-class approval or major pivotal readout in the same indication/target
signals.action: Draft competitive-event brief to PM/BD/sponsor relationship lead; humans assess portfolio impact

signals.name: FX / contract-currency & funding-tranche exposure
signals.signal: Macro-financial exposure that can squeeze or stall the contract: adverse FX movement on a cross-currency contract (sponsor and CRO in different currencies) eroding budget, or a sponsor financing event at risk — biotech runway tied to a pending raise/IPO/grant/partnership tranche, milestone-funding gate slipping, or going-concern/auditor warning. Complements AR-aging and runway with the currency and funding-trigger dimension.
signals.engine: Cloud (FX feeds, sponsor financial disclosures) — non-sensitive commercial
signals.data_source: FX rates vs contract baseline, sponsor SEC/financial filings, funding/milestone tranche schedule, going-concern disclosures
signals.detection: FX move beyond contract tolerance band; missed/at-risk funding tranche; going-concern flag; covenant breach
signals.threshold: FX deviation beyond agreed band; any funding tranche slip or going-concern disclosure
signals.action: Draft financial-exposure alert to finance/PM/account lead; humans manage contract and currency hedging

signals.name: Sponsor-behavior drift (Wasserstein)
signals.signal: W distance of the sponsor's recent engagement / payment / scope distribution vs their historical baseline barycenter — a continuous relationship-drift signal earlier than any single threshold.
signals.data_source: CRM / finance-AR / comms metadata (non-sensitive)
signals.detection: Optimal Transport on engagement features; a rising distance = the relationship cooling.
signals.threshold: W-distance beyond the baseline band
signals.action: Account / BD lead review

signals.name: Program-level free-energy (commercial surprise)
signals.signal: Surprise of the program trajectory (milestones, comms cadence, AR) vs the expected-sponsor model — the top of the hierarchy; an early commercial-risk signal.
signals.data_source: program milestones / comms / AR
signals.detection: Active inference at the client level; decision-support for the account team.
signals.threshold: Sustained free-energy rise
signals.action: Account lead + leadership review

facts: Hybrid model-agnostic gateway routes every signal by data sensitivity: subject-level safety/PK/unblinded data is processed ONLY by an on-device local open-weight LLM (zero egress, runs inside the validated environment); non-sensitive de-identified operational/commercial/portfolio data goes to cloud M365 Copilot Premium. The routing rule is enforced at the connector/data-classification layer, not left to the model.

Governance is constant across the platform: the AI detects, aggregates, triages, explains, and DRAFTS the alert — it never makes the safety, DLT, or termination decision. Validated tools + medical monitor / SRC / DSMB own safety & DLT calls; PM owns study calls; account/BD lead owns client calls. Reported safety numbers always come from the validated safety database, never an LLM.

ICH E6(R3) (adopted 6 Jan 2025) is the RBQM backbone: site-comparable Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) plus trial-level Quality Tolerance Limits (QTLs) — now reframed as broader pre-specified 'acceptable ranges' on critical-to-quality factors — feed the scoring layer; central statistical monitoring (CSM) algorithms detect site anomalies/digit-preference/outliers.

Dose-escalation context drives participant/study tiers: the platform is design-aware (3+3, BOIN, mTPI-2, CRM, accelerated titration, sentinel dosing) so DLT-window and cohort DLT-rate signals are read against the active stopping/de-escalation rule; DLTs are graded by CTCAE and adjudicated by the SRC.

The scoring/aggregation layer rolls individual signals up to a tier RAG (red-amber-green) risk level via transparent WEIGHTED KRIs/QTLs with documented weights and thresholds — it is an explainable triage score with full signal attribution, NOT an autonomous verdict; a human reviews and dispositions every red/amber.

21 CFR Part 11 compliant with ALCOA++ data integrity: every signal, score, AI draft, human acknowledgement, and disposition is captured in an immutable, time-stamped, attributable audit trail with e-signatures and version control.

Refresh cadence is tiered to clinical urgency: near-real-time / event-driven for participant safety (EDC, safety DB, IXRS, labs, ECG), daily for study-operational KRIs (CTMS, query/deviation, Smartsheet), and weekly for client/commercial signals (finance-AR, CRM, sponsor comms, public filings).

Connectors are read-only and validated: EDC, validated safety/PV database, IXRS/RTSM, central-lab, ECG core-lab, CTMS, Smartsheet, finance/AR, CRM, sponsor-comms mailbox, and web for public sponsor filings — each tagged with a sensitivity class that determines local-vs-cloud routing.

signals.name: On-device local LLM backend (sensitive subject-level engine)
signals.signal: Processes all subject-level safety/PK/unblinded data to detect, summarize, and draft alerts for participant- and drill-down study-level signals entirely inside the validated environment
signals.data_source: EDC, validated safety/PV database, central-lab, ECG core-lab, IXRS (subject-level)
signals.detection: Local open-weight LLM runs RAG/summarization over subject records on-device; zero network egress; the model triages and EXPLAINS, validated tools compute the reportable safety numbers and the SRC/medical monitor makes the call
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; sensitivity classification 'subject-level/unblinded' forces local-only processing
signals.action: Drafts subject-level early-warning narratives for the medical monitor / SRC; no data leaves the device

signals.name: Cloud M365 Copilot backend (non-sensitive ops/commercial engine)
signals.signal: Processes de-identified aggregate operational and business/portfolio data to detect, summarize, and draft alerts for study-operational and client/sponsor signals
signals.data_source: CTMS, Smartsheet, finance/AR, CRM, sponsor-comms, web (public filings) — de-identified/aggregate only
signals.detection: Cloud Copilot summarizes and triages non-sensitive KRIs and commercial signals; drafts alerts for PM and account/BD lead; validated tools/CSM compute the metrics
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; only data classified non-sensitive/de-identified is permitted to the cloud
signals.action: Drafts study-ops and commercial early-warning summaries for PM and account/BD lead

signals.name: Sensitivity-based routing gateway
signals.signal: Enforces the local-vs-cloud rule for every record: subject-level safety/PK/unblinded -> on-device local; non-sensitive ops/commercial -> cloud Copilot
signals.data_source: Data-classification metadata on every connector
signals.detection: Rule-based gateway at the connector layer tags each record's sensitivity class and routes accordingly before any model sees it; routing decisions are logged
signals.engine: Validated tool + human (gateway policy; AI does not choose its own route)
signals.threshold: n/a — deterministic policy rule (sensitivity class = local-only vs cloud-eligible)
signals.action: Blocks any subject-level/unblinded payload from reaching the cloud; logs the routing decision to the Part-11 audit trail

signals.name: Validated data connectors (read-only ingestion)
signals.signal: Ingests source data from all trial and business systems to feed the signal engines and the validated scoring tools
signals.data_source: EDC, validated safety DB, IXRS/RTSM, central-lab, ECG core-lab, CTMS, Smartsheet, finance/AR, CRM, sponsor-comms, web (public filings)
signals.detection: Read-only, validated, time-stamped pulls with each feed tagged by sensitivity class; reportable numbers sourced from validated systems, not the LLM
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; connector validation and ALCOA++ lineage required
signals.action: Feeds the scoring/aggregation layer and the appropriate (local/cloud) engine

signals.name: 3-tier RAG dashboard view (Participant / Study / Client)
signals.signal: Presents red-amber-green status per tier with top-signals, trend sparklines, and drill-down from tier risk down to the contributing signal and source record
signals.data_source: Scoring/aggregation layer (all connectors)
signals.detection: Renders weighted-KRI roll-up as RAG tiles; sparklines show signal trajectory over time; drill-down routes subject-level detail back through the on-device engine
signals.engine: Validated tool + human (rendering of local + cloud outputs)
signals.threshold: n/a — display layer; RAG bands inherited from the scoring layer's KRI/QTL thresholds
signals.action: Gives the medical monitor/SRC, PM, and account lead a single triage surface to review and disposition

signals.name: Scoring & aggregation layer (weighted-KRI roll-up)
signals.signal: Rolls individual signals up to a per-tier RAG risk level using transparent, documented weighted KRIs/QTLs with full signal attribution
signals.data_source: All signal feeds (participant, study, client)
signals.detection: Statistical/rule-based weighted scoring (config-controlled weights and thresholds); explainable — every score links back to its contributing signals; it is triage support, NOT an autonomous verdict
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; per-tier KRI weights and QTL/'acceptable-range' bands are pre-specified and version-controlled
signals.action: Sets tier RAG status; any red/amber requires human review and disposition

signals.name: Alerting & escalation routing
signals.signal: Routes each drafted alert to the correct owner by domain: safety -> medical monitor / SRC / DSMB; study-ops -> PM; client/commercial -> account/BD lead
signals.data_source: Scoring/aggregation layer + signal metadata
signals.detection: Rule-based routing on signal category and severity; AI drafts the alert text and rationale, the human owner triages and decides
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; escalation tier triggered by the signal's RAG band and category
signals.action: Delivers the right alert to the right decision-maker with SLA tracking

signals.name: Human acknowledgement & disposition log
signals.signal: Captures the named human's review, acknowledgement, and disposition (e.g., escalate / monitor / close / route to SRC) for every alert
signals.data_source: Alert workflow
signals.detection: Each alert requires an attributable, time-stamped human disposition with rationale before close; enforces 'AI flags, human decides'
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; no red/amber alert closes without a recorded human disposition
signals.action: Creates the accountable record that a human owned the call

signals.name: 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail (ALCOA++)
signals.signal: Records every signal, score, AI draft, routing decision, acknowledgement, and disposition as an immutable, attributable, time-stamped entry
signals.data_source: All platform actions and engine outputs
signals.detection: Rule-based logging with e-signatures and version control; ALCOA++ data lineage; tamper-evident
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; Part-11 / ALCOA++ compliance required for all entries
signals.action: Provides the regulatory-inspectable record of detection-to-disposition

signals.name: Tiered refresh cadence
signals.signal: Updates each tier at a cadence matched to clinical urgency
signals.data_source: Per-connector schedules / event triggers
signals.detection: Near-real-time / event-driven for participant safety (EDC, safety DB, IXRS, labs, ECG); daily for study-ops KRIs (CTMS, queries, deviations, Smartsheet); weekly for client/commercial (finance-AR, CRM, sponsor comms, public filings)
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; cadence set per data domain
signals.action: Ensures safety signals surface fast while commercial signals refresh on a fit-for-purpose cycle

signals.name: Explainability & source-citation layer
signals.signal: Every AI-drafted alert cites its source records and the rule/KRI that fired, so reviewers can verify against the validated system
signals.data_source: All connectors + scoring layer
signals.detection: RAG with mandatory source citation; the model explains WHY a signal fired and links to source; reviewer confirms against the validated safety DB
signals.engine: On-device local LLM (subject-level) / Cloud M365 Copilot (non-sensitive)
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; no uncited safety claim is shown as fact
signals.action: Lets the human owner trust-but-verify before dispositioning

signals.name: Role-based access & blinding controls
signals.signal: Restricts unblinded/subject-level views to authorized roles (medical monitor, DSMB, unblinded statistician) and keeps the blinded team blinded
signals.data_source: Identity/access management + sensitivity tags
signals.detection: Rule-based RBAC and blinding firewall; the on-device engine and unblinded panels are gated to authorized roles only
signals.threshold: n/a — capability; access tied to authorized role and blinding status
signals.action: Preserves trial integrity and blinding while still surfacing tier-appropriate signals

signals.name: Pooled reference priors — Wasserstein barycenters (empirical-Bayes pooling)
signals.signal: The system maintains Wasserstein barycenters of prior cohorts/studies as the reference priors each tier compares against; higher tiers learn the lower tiers' priors (empirical Bayes), so a new cohort/study starts with population-informed expectations and recalibrates as studies complete.
signals.data_source: historical cohorts/studies (per-tier sensitivity)
signals.detection: OT barycenters + hierarchical / empirical-Bayes priors updated across studies — the cross-study pooling loop that sets every tier's reference.
signals.threshold: n/a
signals.action: Sets each tier's reference; recalibrated as studies complete

signals.name: Nested (deep) active-inference hierarchy — participant → study → client
signals.signal: A hierarchical generative model: each tier's beliefs are top-down priors for the tier below; bottom-up prediction errors (surprise) propagate up across timescales (fast participant → slow client). Free energy is minimized jointly, so a participant DLT mechanically raises study- and program-level risk.
signals.data_source: all tiers
signals.detection: Deep / hierarchical active inference; a Wasserstein-regularized free energy ties the levels and the pooled priors together.
signals.threshold: n/a
signals.action: The nested loop; surfaces tier-coupled early warnings
